Bug / Task Description: VectorTimestamp.Compare in pkg/clock/vector.go must return Concurrent whenever neither timestamp dominates the other. When each timestamp carries entries for nodes the other has never seen (disjoint or partially-overlapping key sets where each side leads on different nodes), it currently misclassifies the pair as a clean happens-before/after ordering instead of Concurrent, so causally-independent events get falsely ordered and conflict detection downstream is corrupted. Make Compare detect concurrency correctly for these cases.
Commit Subject: fix(clock): vector clock returns Concurrent for disjoint keys
